questions.q: Whose eldest daughter was Amba?
options: The king of Kashi
The king of Madra
The king of Panchala
The king of Sindhu
questions.explanation: Amba was the eldest of the three daughters of the king of Kashi, ahead of her sisters Ambika and Ambalika.

questions.q: Who carried Amba away from her swayamvara?
options: Duryodhana
Jayadratha
Bhishma
Shalva
questions.explanation: Bhishma abducted Amba and her two sisters from the swayamvara at Kashi, defeating all the assembled suitors.

questions.q: For whose marriage was Amba abducted?
options: Chitrangada, the Kuru king
Vichitravirya, the young king of Hastinapura
Pandu, the pale prince
Shantanu, the aging king
questions.explanation: Bhishma seized the princesses as brides for his half-brother Vichitravirya, since his own vow barred him from marriage.

questions.q: Which king had Amba already chosen in her heart?
options: Drupada of Panchala
Virata of Matsya
Subala of Gandhara
Shalva of Saubha
questions.explanation: Amba had secretly chosen King Shalva of Saubha, and Bhishma released her when she confessed her love.

questions.q: What did Shalva do when Amba returned to him?
options: He married her in a quiet rite
He demanded ransom from Bhishma
He refused her as a prize another man had won
He hid her away to avoid Bhishma's wrath
questions.explanation: Wounded in pride by his defeat, Shalva rejected Amba, saying he could not accept a woman whom Bhishma had carried off.

questions.q: With every door closed to her, what single purpose did Amba embrace?
options: The destruction of Bhishma
The conquest of Hastinapura
The humbling of King Shalva
The crown of Kashi for herself
questions.explanation: Rejected by Shalva and unable to wed Vichitravirya or Bhishma, Amba dedicated her life to Bhishma's destruction.

questions.q: Which god finally promised Amba her revenge?
options: Vishnu
Indra
Agni
Shiva
questions.explanation: After years of austerities, Shiva appeared to Amba and promised that she would cause Bhishma's death.

questions.q: When would Amba's revenge upon Bhishma be fulfilled?
options: Within a year of the god's promise
In her next birth, in another body
On the day Bhishma broke his vow
At the moment of her own death
questions.explanation: Shiva's boon was that Amba would slay Bhishma not in her present life but in her next birth.

questions.q: How did Amba end her first life?
options: She walked into a blazing fire she had built
She drowned herself in the Ganga
She fasted to death at a shrine of Shiva
She fell on a sword before Bhishma's gates
questions.explanation: Impatient for her promised revenge, Amba built a pyre and walked into the flames to hasten her rebirth.

questions.q: As whom was Amba reborn?
options: Draupadi, the fire-born queen of Panchala
Uttara, the young princess of Matsya
Shikhandi, a child of King Drupada
Hidimbi, the queen of the forest
questions.explanation: Amba was reborn as Shikhandini in Drupada's house, raised as a son and later known as Shikhandi.

questions.q: Who were Amba's two younger sisters?
options: Sudeshna and Uttaraa
Kripi and Devika
Madri and Kausalya
Ambika and Ambalika
questions.explanation: Her sisters Ambika and Ambalika married Vichitravirya and later became the mothers of Dhritarashtra and Pandu.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma let Amba leave Hastinapura while keeping her sisters?
options: The king of Kashi paid a ransom for her alone
She confessed that her heart was pledged to Shalva
An omen warned that she would doom the marriage
Vichitravirya found her too proud to wed
questions.explanation: When Amba revealed she had already chosen Shalva, Bhishma honorably sent her to him, an act that doomed her instead.

questions.q: Which great warrior agreed to champion Amba's cause against Bhishma?
options: Parashurama, the slayer of kshatriya kings
Drupada, the proud king of Panchala
Karna, the gifted king of Anga
Balarama, the wielder of the plough
questions.explanation: Parashurama, Bhishma's own teacher in arms, took up Amba's cause and confronted his former student.

questions.q: Through whom did Amba reach Parashurama with her plea?
options: Her father, who rode to the master's mountain
The god Agni, who carried her lament
Her grandfather, the royal sage Hotravahana
Her sister Ambika, by secret letter
questions.explanation: Her maternal grandfather, the royal sage Hotravahana, with Parashurama's follower Akritavrana, brought her case before the master.

questions.q: What did Parashurama first demand of Bhishma on Amba's behalf?
options: That he restore Amba to the throne of Kashi
That he take Amba as his wife
That he renounce his weapons forever
That he kneel and beg Amba's forgiveness
questions.explanation: Parashurama ordered Bhishma to marry the woman his abduction had ruined, and Bhishma's refusal led to their duel.

questions.q: How did the combat between Parashurama and Bhishma end?
options: Parashurama disarmed Bhishma on the final day
Bhishma struck his guru down and wept over him
Amba herself begged them to lay down arms
Neither yielded, and the gods and ancestors stopped the fight
questions.explanation: After twenty-three days neither warrior could prevail, and the celestials and ancestors intervened to end the duel.

questions.q: What path did Amba take after even Parashurama failed her?
options: She returned to Shalva's court to plead once more
She raised an army from the kings of the east
She turned to years of harsh austerities
She entered the service of the goddess Ganga
questions.explanation: With no champion left, Amba undertook terrible austerities for years to win the favor of the gods herself.

questions.q: Amba received a garland of never-fading flowers destined to mark Bhishma's slayer; where did it finally hang when no king dared accept it?
options: On the palace gate of King Drupada
On the door of Bhishma's war tent
On the lance of the king of Saubha
On the chariot of Arjuna at Kurukshetra
questions.explanation: Amba hung the garland on Drupada's gate, and years later Shikhandi took it up; many retellings say Kartikeya gave her the garland.

questions.q: What exactly did Shiva grant Amba after her austerities?
options: Immediate strength to slay Bhishma herself
A celestial weapon no armor could resist
Bhishma's vow would crumble within the year
Rebirth in which she would cause Bhishma's death
questions.explanation: Shiva promised that in her next life she would attain manhood, remember her purpose, and become the cause of Bhishma's fall.

questions.q: Into whose royal house was Amba reborn?
options: Drupada's, king of Panchala
Virata's, king of Matsya
Shalya's, king of Madra
Subala's, king of Gandhara
questions.explanation: Amba was reborn as a daughter of King Drupada of Panchala, who raised the child as a son named Shikhandi.

questions.q: How did the reborn Shikhandini come to possess a male body?
options: Shiva descended and transformed her at the war's eve
A yaksha named Sthunakarna exchanged his sex with hers
She was born male by the direct boon of Agni
Drupada's sacrificial fire reshaped her form
questions.explanation: The yaksha Sthunakarna took pity on the desperate Shikhandini and exchanged his manhood for her womanhood.

questions.q: How was Amba's vengeance finally accomplished at Kurukshetra?
options: Shikhandi's own arrows pierced Bhishma's heart
Shikhandi duelled Bhishma alone for a day and a night
Bhishma dropped his weapons before Shikhandi while Arjuna shot from behind
Shikhandi cursed Bhishma so that his weapons failed
questions.explanation: On the tenth day Bhishma refused to fight one born a woman, and Arjuna, shielded by Shikhandi, shot the grandsire down.

questions.q: According to the Udyoga Parva, what did part of Amba become through her austerities and grief?
options: A flame that burns eternally at Kurukshetra
A crooked river in the Vatsa land, flowing only in the rains
A night-blooming flower on the Yamuna's banks
A shadow bound forever to Bhishma's banner
questions.explanation: The epic says half of Amba became the crooked, seasonal river Amba in the Vatsa country, while half remained a maiden.

questions.q: Who recounts Amba's full story in the epic, and to whom?
options: Vyasa tells it to Janamejaya at the snake sacrifice
Krishna tells it to Arjuna before the first battle
Sanjaya tells it to the blind king Dhritarashtra
Bhishma tells it to Duryodhana, explaining his refusal to fight Shikhandi
questions.explanation: In the Udyoga Parva, Bhishma himself narrates Amba's whole history to Duryodhana to explain why he will not strike Shikhandi.

questions.q: Which divine decree fixed the yaksha Sthunakarna in female form?
options: Kubera ruled he would stay a woman until Shikhandi's death
Indra punished him for surrendering a celestial gift
Shiva bound him to the exchange for a hundred years
Brahma decreed the change could never be reversed
questions.explanation: Kubera, angered at finding Sthunakarna transformed, decreed the yaksha would remain female until Shikhandi died.

questions.q: Why did Parashurama finally lay down arms in the duel fought for Amba?
options: He saw Vishnu's form within Bhishma and bowed
His axe shattered against Bhishma's celestial mail
His ancestors and the celestials commanded him to desist
Amba withdrew her plea, ashamed of the bloodshed
questions.explanation: Parashurama's own ancestors and the gods intervened and bade him stop, and he admitted he could not defeat Bhishma.

questions.q: What crisis did the young Shikhandini's secret nearly cause for Drupada?
options: Panchala's priests demanded her exile from the kingdom
The Kurus annulled their treaty with Panchala
Drupada's queen fled the court with the child
King Hiranyavarman of Dasharna, her father-in-law, marched to war on learning the truth
questions.explanation: Shikhandini had been married to the daughter of Hiranyavarman of Dasharna, who threatened to destroy Panchala when the bride discovered the secret.

questions.q: Where did Amba first seek shelter when both Shalva and Hastinapura were closed to her?
options: The court of her father in Kashi
A hermitage of ascetics, refusing to return to either court
The mountain retreat of Parashurama
The banks of the Ganga, as a wandering pilgrim
questions.explanation: Too proud to return to any court, Amba took refuge with forest ascetics, where her grandfather Hotravahana found her.

questions.q: In which river's waters is Amba said to have stood during her terrible austerities?
options: The Ganga
The Saraswati
The Yamuna
The Godavari
questions.explanation: The epic describes her standing for a year in the waters of the Yamuna among other extreme penances.

questions.q: With what declaration did Amba enter the fire at the end of her first life?
options: That her death was for Bhishma's destruction
That she forgave Shalva but never Bhishma
That she would rise as a goddess of vengeance
That the Kuru line would perish root and branch
questions.explanation: As she entered the flames, Amba declared that she died for Bhishma's destruction, sealing her single purpose into her rebirth.
